title: achillea oxyloba subsp. barrelieri
intro: Achillea oxyloba subsp. barrelieri, also known as the Barrelier's yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Barrelier's yarrow include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy. Barrelier's yarrow can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
